title: inga cochliacarpos
intro: Inga cochliacarpos is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the "inca peanut" due to its peanut-like pods. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Inga cochliacarpos include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Inga cochliacarpos is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its seeds can be roasted as a substitute for peanuts.
